files.patch:
@@ -50,7 +50,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-4943","title":"Add missing options to agent snapshot command","description":"## Task
Add missing options to `pt agent snapshot` command per CLI specification.

## Current Implementation
```
pt agent snapshot [OPTIONS]
  --label <LABEL>    Label for the snapshot
```

## Missing Options (per CLI_SPECIFICATION.md:491-506)
```
--top <N>            Limit processes per category (default: 10)
--include-env        Include environment variable summaries
--include-network    Include network connection information
```

## Implementation

### AgentSnapshotArgs struct changes
```rust
#[derive(Args, Debug)]
struct AgentSnapshotArgs {
    /// Label for the snapshot
    #[arg(long)]
    label: Option<String>,
    
    /// Limit to top N processes by resource usage (CPU+memory)
    #[arg(long)]
    top: Option<usize>,
    
    /// Include environment variable summaries (redacted)
    #[arg(long)]
    include_env: bool,
    
    /// Include network connection information (requires lsof/ss)
    #[arg(long)]
    include_network: bool,
}
```

### --top Behavior
Limit resource_hogs categories to top N entries:
```rust
let top_limit = args.top.unwrap_or(10);
resource_hogs.top_cpu.truncate(top_limit);
resource_hogs.top_memory.truncate(top_limit);
resource_hogs.top_io.truncate(top_limit);
```

### --include-env Behavior
Add environment section with REDACTED variable values:
```rust
if args.include_env {
    let env_summary = collect_env_summary(&processes);
    // Values are hashed/redacted, only var names preserved
    // Common vars: PATH, HOME, USER, SHELL, etc.
}
```

Output:
```json
{
  "environment": {
    "summary": "42 unique env vars across 12 processes",
    "common_vars": ["PATH", "HOME", "USER", "SHELL", "TERM"],
    "notable": [
      {"var": "DEBUG", "process_count": 5},
      {"var": "NODE_ENV", "process_count": 3, "values_redacted": true}
    ]
  }
}
```

### --include-network Behavior

#### Tool Precedence
Network information collection follows this precedence:
1. **`ss` (preferred)**: Faster, included in modern Linux (iproute2)
2. **`lsof`**: Fallback, more portable but slower
3. **`/proc/net`**: Last resort, manual parsing

```rust
fn collect_network_info() -> NetworkInfo {
    // Try ss first (fastest, most reliable on Linux)
    if let Ok(info) = collect_via_ss() {
        return NetworkInfo { source: "ss", ..info };
    }
    
    // Fallback to lsof
    if let Ok(info) = collect_via_lsof() {
        return NetworkInfo { source: "lsof", ..info };
    }
    
    // Last resort: /proc/net parsing
    if let Ok(info) = collect_via_proc_net() {
        return NetworkInfo { source: "proc_net", ..info };
    }
    
    // No tools available
    NetworkInfo {
        available: false,
        source: "none",
        error: Some("Network tools unavailable (tried: ss, lsof, /proc/net)".into()),
        ..Default::default()
    }
}
```

#### Output when tools available:
```json
{
  "network": {
    "available": true,
    "source": "ss",
    "listening_ports": [
      {"port": 3000, "proto": "tcp", "pid": 1234, "comm": "node"},
      {"port": 5432, "proto": "tcp", "pid": 567, "comm": "postgres"}
    ],
    "established_connections": [
      {"local": "127.0.0.1:3000", "remote": "127.0.0.1:45678", "pid": 1234, "state": "ESTABLISHED"}
    ],
    "stats": {
      "total_listening": 5,
      "total_established": 23,
      "total_time_wait": 12
    }
  }
}
```

#### Output when tools unavailable:
```json
{
  "network": {
    "available": false,
    "source": "none",
    "error": "Network tools unavailable (tried: ss, lsof, /proc/net)",
    "install_hint": "Install iproute2 for ss or lsof: apt install iproute2 lsof"
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--top N` limits category lists to N entries
- [ ] `--top` without value uses default (10)
- [ ] `--include-env` adds environment section with redacted values
- [ ] `--include-network` adds network section
- [ ] Default behavior unchanged (top=10 implicit, no env/network)
- [ ] Missing network tools gracefully handled (shows "unavailable" with hint)
- [ ] Network source field indicates which tool was used
- [ ] Help text documents all options

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_snapshot_top_limits_output() {
    // --top 5 limits each category to 5 entries
}

#[test]
fn test_snapshot_default_top_is_ten() {
    // Without --top, categories limited to 10
}

#[test]
fn test_snapshot_env_redacts_values() {
    // Environment values are redacted/hashed, not raw
}

#[test]
fn test_snapshot_network_tool_precedence() {
    // ss tried first, then lsof, then /proc/net
}

#[test]
fn test_snapshot_network_unavailable_graceful() {
    // When no tools available, returns structured error not panic
}
```

### Integration Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_snapshot_with_all_options() {
    // --top 5 --include-env --include-network
    // Verify all sections present and correctly sized
}

#[test]
fn test_snapshot_label_preserved() {
    // --label "baseline" appears in output
}

#[test]
fn test_snapshot_network_shows_source() {
    // network.source field indicates which tool was used
}
```

### E2E Test Script (test/e2e_snapshot_options.sh)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# E2E test for agent snapshot options

set -euo pipefail
LOG="test/logs/e2e_snapshot_options_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
mkdir -p test/logs

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== E2E Test: snapshot options ==="

# Test basic snapshot (baseline)
log "Testing basic snapshot..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent snapshot --format json 2>&1)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".session_id" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ Basic snapshot works"
else
    log "✗ Basic snapshot failed"
    echo "$OUTPUT" >> "$LOG"
    exit 1
fi

# Test --top option
log "Testing --top option..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent snapshot --top 3 --format json 2>&1)
TOP_CPU_COUNT=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq ".resource_hogs.top_cpu | length // 0" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
if [ "$TOP_CPU_COUNT" -le 3 ]; then
    log "✓ --top 3 limits output (got $TOP_CPU_COUNT entries)"
else
    log "✗ --top 3 should limit to 3, got $TOP_CPU_COUNT"
    exit 1
fi

# Test --include-env option
log "Testing --include-env option..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent snapshot --include-env --format json 2>&1)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".environment" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ --include-env adds environment section"
    
    # Verify structure
    if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".environment.summary" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "✓ Environment has summary field"
    fi
    if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".environment.common_vars" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "✓ Environment has common_vars field"
    fi
else
    log "✗ --include-env should add environment section"
    exit 1
fi

# Test --include-network option
log "Testing --include-network option..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent snapshot --include-network --format json 2>&1)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".network" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ --include-network adds network section"
    
    # Check if tools were available
    AVAILABLE=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r ".network.available // false")
    SOURCE=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r ".network.source // \"unknown\"")
    
    if [ "$AVAILABLE" = "true" ]; then
        log "✓ Network info available via $SOURCE"
        
        LISTENING=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq ".network.stats.total_listening // 0")
        log "  Found $LISTENING listening ports"
    else
        # Graceful degradation - not a failure
        ERROR=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r ".network.error // \"unknown error\"")
        log "⚠ Network info unavailable: $ERROR"
        
        # Verify hint is provided
        if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".network.install_hint" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
            log "✓ Install hint provided"
        fi
    fi
else
    log "✗ --include-network should add network section"
    exit 1
fi

# Test --label option
log "Testing --label option..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent snapshot --label "test-baseline" --format json 2>&1)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".label == \"test-baseline\"" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ --label preserved in output"
else
    log "✗ --label should be preserved"
    exit 1
fi

# Test combined options
log "Testing combined options..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent snapshot --top 5 --include-env --include-network --label "combo" --format json 2>&1)

# Verify environment present
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".environment" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ Environment section present in combo"
else
    log "✗ Environment missing in combo"
    exit 1
fi

# Verify label present
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".label == \"combo\"" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ Label preserved in combo"
else
    log "✗ Label missing in combo"
    exit 1
fi

# Verify top limit applied
TOP_COUNT=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq ".resource_hogs.top_cpu | length // 0" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
if [ "$TOP_COUNT" -le 5 ]; then
    log "✓ Top limit respected in combo (got $TOP_COUNT)"
else
    log "✗ Top limit not respected in combo"
    exit 1
fi

log "=== E2E Test PASSED ==="

 {"id":"bd-5020","title":"E2E: telemetry status + prune/retention","description":"## Goal
Validate telemetry maintenance commands with real outputs and JSONL logging, including failure paths.

## Scope
- `pt telemetry status` verifies counts/sizes.
- `pt telemetry prune` with dry-run + real prune paths.
- Validate retention boundaries and artifact manifest.
- Failure paths: unreadable telemetry dir, invalid retention values.
- Validate exit codes and user-facing error messages.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs: event, cmd, result_counts, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifacts: status outputs, prune report, stderr snapshots.
- Log redaction checks (no secrets/path leakage).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Telemetry status/prune complete deterministically in CI.
- [ ] Retention logic validated with fixture telemetry data.
- [ ] Failure paths produce clear errors + correct exit codes.
- [ ] JSONL logs emitted per case.

## Test Plan
- E2E script prepares fixture telemetry dir then runs status/prune.

-{"id":"bd-dg03","title":"E2E: TUI workflows with PTY recording + JSONL logs","description":"## Goal
Provide end-to-end TUI test scripts with PTY capture and detailed JSONL logging.

## Scope / Subtasks
- PTY harness to drive TUI navigation and keybindings deterministically.
- Record terminal output (asciinema/script) with timestamps.
- JSONL logs: inputs, focus changes, selected actions, exit codes, render timings.
- Validate key TUI flows: open, search/filter, drilldown, toggle actions, apply/abort, exit.
- Flake control: stable terminal size, deterministic seed, timeout watchdogs.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL schema fields: event, timestamp, key, widget, action, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- PTY recordings (asciinema) and screenshots per major screen.
- Artifact manifest includes recordings + logs + summary.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TUI E2E runs deterministically in CI.
- [ ] Logs and screen recordings stored as artifacts.
- [ ] Flows cover apply/abort and drilldown navigation.
- [ ] JSONL schema validation passes.

## Test Plan
- E2E: scripted PTY run with recorded artifacts.
- Logging: JSONL schema validation + artifact manifest validation.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:21.458125743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:18.474955263Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dg03","depends_on_id":"bd-jg2d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:18.468590124Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-dg03","depends_on_id":"process_triage-2ka.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:21.482007393Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-dg03","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:21.467150668Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-dg03","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:22:29.171194703Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-dg03","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:21.474610544Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-hwvl","title":"No-mock session/plan/verify/resume tests","description":"## Goal
Exercise session lifecycle, plan generation, apply/verify, and resumability **without mocks**.

## Scope / Subtasks
- Session creation, manifest/context persistence, and state transitions.
- Plan generation with real `DecisionBundle` fixtures.
- Apply/verify identity revalidation (TOCTOU protection) using real processes.
- Resume partially-applied plans and verify idempotency.
- Cross-check plan schema against agent contract outputs.
- PID reuse scenarios: boot_id mismatch, start_time mismatch, recycled PIDs.
- Snapshot artifact persistence integrity: hash generation, tamper detection, and serialization-failure paths (e.g. NaN) fail closed.

## No-mock Policy (Commentary)
- Use real filesystem sessions under temp dirs.
- Use real child processes for identity/TOCTOU tests.
- Do **not** mock session store, plan builder, pre-checks, or executor.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs for state transitions, action_ids, verify outcomes, and resume status.
- Log schema fields: event, timestamp, session_id, phase, command, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifact manifests list session dirs + plan JSON + verify outputs for CI upload.

## E2E Coverage
- Add `test/session_lifecycle_e2e.bats`: plan -> apply -> resume -> verify with real processes.
- E2E uses runner harness (process_triage-aii.7.7) and emits JSONL logs using the shared schema.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session lifecycle covers Created -> Planned -> Executing -> Completed paths.
- [ ] Identity revalidation fails closed on PID reuse and mismatch scenarios.
- [ ] Resumability is deterministic and idempotent.
- [ ] Schema validation for plan/verify outputs passes.
- [ ] Snapshot artifacts fail closed on serialization errors; integrity hash validated on load.
- [ ] JSONL logs emitted with stable schema.
- [ ] E2E lifecycle run validates outputs and artifacts.

## Test Plan
- Integration: spawn real processes; validate plan/apply/verify.
- E2E: plan -> apply -> resume -> verify (no mocks).
- Logging: JSONL schema validation + artifact manifest.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:12.846956563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T06:48:03.425098753Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:48:03.425034583Z","close_reason":"Complete: session_lifecycle_nomock.rs has 5 tests covering full lifecycle (Created->Planned->Executing->Completed), plan generation with DecisionBundle fixtures, verify with real processes (TOCTOU), resume with identity revalidation, PID reuse/mismatch, process-gone detection. All tests pass with --features test-utils.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwvl","depends_on_id":"bd-jg2d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:30.354821944Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-hwvl","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:12.859210660Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-hwvl","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:12.864241301Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-hwvl","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:22:23.666137713Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-hwvl","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:45:26.514790492Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-jg2d","title":"Define E2E artifact manifest spec + validator","description":"## Goal
Define a **shared E2E artifact manifest spec** and validation tooling so every test emits consistent, machine‑readable artifacts.

## Scope / Subtasks
- Define a versioned manifest schema (JSON) with required fields:
  - schema_version, run_id, suite, test_id, timestamp
  - env (os, arch, kernel, ci provider, capabilities)
  - commands[] (argv, exit_code, duration_ms)
  - logs[] (path, kind=jsonl/text, sha256, bytes)
  - artifacts[] (path, kind, sha256, bytes, redaction_profile)
  - metrics (timings, counts, flake_retries)
- Provide validator CLI utility (or test helper) that:
  - validates schema
  - verifies file existence and checksums
  - enforces redaction profile field when required
- Document manifest layout and include examples in `test/fixtures/manifest_examples/`.
- Add unit tests for schema validation and checksum verification.

## Logging & Artifacts
- Manifest itself is an artifact and must be hashed.
- JSONL logs must reference the manifest `run_id` for correlation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manifest schema is versioned and documented.
- [ ] Validator rejects missing/invalid fields and bad checksums.
- [ ] Examples exist for TUI, install, daemon, and bundle/report E2E runs.
- [ ] Unit tests cover schema evolution (backward compatible changes).

## Test Plan
- Unit: schema validation + checksum verification.
- Integration: generate a manifest for a sample run and validate it.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:01.907912841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T08:41:56.265478358Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T08:41:56.265385806Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jg2d","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:01.921060778Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-jg2d","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:09.679304299Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-jg2d","depends_on_id":"process_triage-f5o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:29.324238179Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-js27","title":"Fix flaky shadow tests via ENV_LOCK mutex","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"StormyMarsh","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:32:50.615627896Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:33:51.813269111Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T23:33:51.813198861Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flaky","shadow","test"]}
